---
title: "LRP77a: Dose-response (ability-adjusted sensitivity) — tests no `g -> dose`"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
```

## Overview

LRP77a is the **ability-adjusted sensitivity fit** for [LRP77](../lrp77/). It is
the identical period-resolved dose-response model with the **baseline-skill
cluster** added to the adjustment set: letter-sound knowledge ($L$), expressive
vocabulary ($E$) and phoneme blending ($B$), each as its baseline (pre) logit.
These are the reflective indicators of latent general ability $g$ in the locked
DAG, alongside the already-adjusted baseline reading $W_{\text{pre}}$.

**Why.** The locked DAG omits $g \to \text{dose}$ ($GA \to IS$), but if abler children attended more
(Frank 2012) then $g$ is a common cause of dose and outcome and
$\{G, W_{\text{pre}}, A\}$ no longer identifies the dose effect — and because dose
is **not** randomised, randomisation does not rescue it. Conditioning on the
baseline-skill cluster blocks that path (only *pre*-dose skills, never anything
downstream of dose).

**How to read it — against LRP77.** If the dose slope is materially unchanged
here, the no-`ability -> dose` assumption is defensible in this sample. If it
collapses toward zero, the LRP77 dose signal is substantially ability-confounded
and should be downgraded accordingly. (Requiring $L/E/B$ baselines complete-cases
marginally fewer rows than LRP77.)

See the LRP77 report for the full estimand, the $G = 2 - \text{group}$ coding (G=1 = intervention), and the
expectations caveat (adjusted association; $R^2$ 0.1–0.3 in Phase 1).

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}
![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Dose slope (ability-adjusted)

```{python}
# | echo: false
dose = pd.read_csv(_here / "dose_slope_summary.csv")
dose
```

Compare `dose_overall` / `dose_period*` here with the same rows in the LRP77
report. The baseline-skill coefficients (`gamma_L_pre`, `gamma_E_pre`,
`gamma_B_pre`) appear in the diagnostics table above.

## Posterior predictive

![Posterior predictive check](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
